title: Buttery Crispy Korean Bread Bao
description: A delicious and crispy Korean bread bao recipe, perfect for a quick snack or meal. Features a crispy double-rise-bread exterior and a buttery, flaky interior with a hint of mustard seeds.

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Use a high-protein flour for a chewier texture

ingredients.name: Whole wheat flour
ingredients.notes: Add some fiber and nutrition to your bao

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for a more nuanced flavor

ingredients.name: Sugar
ingredients.notes: A small amount of sugar helps balance the savory flavors

ingredients.name: Dry yeast
ingredients.notes: Active dry yeast works best for this recipe

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: Unsalted butter for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Use warm water for a faster rise

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: For brushing the bao during baking

ingredients.name: Black mustard seeds
ingredients.notes: Add some heat and flavor to your bao

ingredients.name: Sesame seeds
ingredients.notes: Optional, but adds a nice nutty flavor and crunch

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: For egg wash and brushing the bao

ingredients.name: Scallions
ingredients.notes: Optional, for added flavor and color

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, whole wheat flour, salt, and sugar.
Add the dry yeast and mix until the yeast is fully incorporated.
Gradually add the warm water and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place the disks on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each bao.
Brush the tops with egg wash and sprinkle with sesame seeds, if using.
Bake the bao for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy.
While the bao is baking, melt the butter and brush it onto the bao during the last 2 minutes of baking.
Remove the bao from the oven and let them cool for 5 minutes before serving.
